headline: TomTom's reorganization boosts operating profit in Q1
summary: Dutch digital mapping specialist TomTom reported on Thursday higher ‌than expected operating profit for ‌the first-quarter of the year despite lower revenue ​following an internal reorganization.  Its earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT) came in at 13.8 million euros ($16.3 million), above analysts' average estimate ‌of 7 ⁠million euros in a company-provided consensus and last year's level ⁠of 5.7 million euros.  TomTom said the growth was driven by a higher margin ​and lower ​operating expenses ​after completing a ‌reorganization last year.

content.title: TomTom's reorganization boosts operating profit in Q1
content.summary: Dutch digital mapping specialist TomTom reported on Thursday higher ‌than expected operating profit for ‌the first-quarter of the year despite lower revenue ​following an internal reorganization.  Its earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT) came in at 13.8 million euros ($16.3 million), above analysts' average estimate ‌of 7 ⁠million euros in a company-provided consensus and last year's level ⁠of 5.7 million euros.  TomTom said the growth was driven by a higher margin ​and lower ​operating expenses ​after completing a ‌reorganization last year.

headline: Still Looking for the Next Warren Buffett? We Found ‘Him.’
summary: As Warren Buffett exits the stage, it’s reasonable to ask: Who will become the next ultimate sage of American business?  First, we’re coming up on  Berkshire Hathaway’s  annual meeting on Saturday, May 2, where for the first time in 60 years, Buffett, 95—arguably the leading voice of American business for decades—won’t be on stage leading the flock.  Because Buffett’s skill set is so multidimensional, filling his shoes is almost like replacing a combination of Michael Jordan, Tiger Woods, and Wayne Gretzky.

content.title: Still Looking for the Next Warren Buffett? We Found ‘Him.’
content.summary: As Warren Buffett exits the stage, it’s reasonable to ask: Who will become the next ultimate sage of American business?  First, we’re coming up on  Berkshire Hathaway’s  annual meeting on Saturday, May 2, where for the first time in 60 years, Buffett, 95—arguably the leading voice of American business for decades—won’t be on stage leading the flock.  Because Buffett’s skill set is so multidimensional, filling his shoes is almost like replacing a combination of Michael Jordan, Tiger Woods, and Wayne Gretzky.

headline: Stellantis, Microsoft sign five-year partnership for AI push
summary: Stellantis and Microsoft said on Thursday they agreed to a five‑year strategic partnership to co-develop artificial intelligence (AI), cybersecurity and engineering ‌capabilities, as the automaker races to keep pace with technology-focused rivals.  Software and ‌data-driven services have become central in the automotive companies' long-term strategies, especially as Chinese automakers accelerate their ​development of features to entice customers domestically and abroad.  Legacy automakers, which have often struggled to master software and tech efforts on their own, are increasingly looking to partner with tech companies to leverage their expertise and speed.

content.title: Stellantis, Microsoft sign five-year partnership for AI push
content.summary: Stellantis and Microsoft said on Thursday they agreed to a five‑year strategic partnership to co-develop artificial intelligence (AI), cybersecurity and engineering ‌capabilities, as the automaker races to keep pace with technology-focused rivals.  Software and ‌data-driven services have become central in the automotive companies' long-term strategies, especially as Chinese automakers accelerate their ​development of features to entice customers domestically and abroad.  Legacy automakers, which have often struggled to master software and tech efforts on their own, are increasingly looking to partner with tech companies to leverage their expertise and speed.

Shares of chip giant Nvidia (NVDA) are on a tear in the month of April after a wobbly March.

Great Hill Capital chairman Thomas Hayes joins Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré to examine Nvidia's current position in the AI semiconductor space.

headline: Oracle’s New AWS Partnership Just Put It Ahead of Azure and Google Cloud

headline: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 Mark New Highs; These Truck Stocks Clear Entries (Live Coverage)
summary: The Nasdaq composite and other major indexes regained upward momentum Thursday, closing a seesaw session higher as Wall Street awaited a pending ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon.  Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing fell 3% on the stock market today following its earnings report but stayed near the high of its current base.  Meanwhile, data-center chip innovator Advanced Micro Devices broke out and rallied nearly 8% in heavy turnover.

content.title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 Mark New Highs; These Truck Stocks Clear Entries (Live Coverage)

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing fell 3% on the stock market today following its earnings report but stayed near the high of its current base.  Meanwhile, data-center chip innovator Advanced Micro Devices broke out and rallied nearly 8% in heavy turnover.

headline: Is Microsoft (MSFT) Quietly Turning Industrial Cloud Tie-Ups Like Stellantis Into Its Next Moat?

This broadening alliance places Microsoft deeper inside Stellantis’ vehicle, manufacturing, and IT stack, reinforcing Azure and Copilot as core platforms for...
